title: Task 0: Welcome to build-with-chipi — read before starting
body:
**Hey May!** Before you build anything, I want you to understand what we're doing and why your work matters.

## What is Chipi?

Chipi is infrastructure for building fintech, marketplaces, e-commerce, or any app that needs wallets and payments. We handle:
- **Wallet creation** with passkeys (no seed phrases, no passwords)
- **Gasless transactions** (users never pay gas fees, we sponsor them)
- **Session keys** (apps can execute transactions autonomously)
- **x402 payments** (APIs that charge per call, like a toll booth)
- **SKU purchases** (phone recharges, gift cards, bill payments)

Any developer in the world can install Chipi, create wallets for their users, and send payments — all on Starknet mainnet, with real USDC. Zero fees for them.

## Our repositories

| Repo | What it does | You'll interact with it when... |
|------|-------------|-------------------------------|
| `chipi-pay/sdks` | The SDK monorepo — all npm packages + Python SDK + docs | You find a bug in a hook, a docs page is wrong, or an install fails |
| `chipi-pay/chipi-back` | Backend API — wallets, transactions, SKUs | An API call returns an error or unexpected data |
| `chipi-pay/build-with-chipi` | THIS repo — tutorials, templates, CLI | Where your PRs go |
| `chipi-pay/x402-facilitator` | x402 payment protocol server | x402 payment flow fails |
| `chipi-pay/pwa-wallet` | The Chipi wallet app (you worked on this before) | Reference only — you won't push to it |
| `chipi-pay/backend-dashboard` | Admin dashboard for orgs | Reference only |

## How to file a bug

When something doesn't work while following the docs:
1. Go to the **correct repo** (see table above)
2. Click "New Issue" → "Bug Report"
3. Fill in: package name, what the docs said to do, what actually happened
4. Link the bug back to your tutorial issue in build-with-chipi

The bug templates are already set up. Use them. Every bug you find makes the platform better for every developer worldwide.

## Our business model

Chipi makes money when developers use our platform. More developers → more orgs → more transactions → more revenue. To unlock this growth we need battle-tested tools that just work. Comment **chipi-builder** on this issue when you're ready to start.

- **x402 transactions** — we take a tiny fee on every API payment
- **Active orgs** — more orgs on the dashboard = more wallets = more transactions
- **SKU purchases** — commission on phone recharges, gift cards, etc.

## What you're building and why

Your tutorials serve FOUR purposes:
1. **QA** — you're the first person to test every docs page from scratch. Bugs you find = bugs users don't hit.
2. **Content** — your recordings become marketing. Developers see Chipi working and want to build with it.
3. **Templates** — your projects become CLI templates. `npx create-chipi-app` scaffolds from YOUR code.
4. **Coverage** — every tutorial updates our coverage map. We know exactly what works and what doesn't.

tutorial → template → CLI → developers → orgs → revenue → growth

## Quality in fintech/web3

We handle real money on a real blockchain. There's no "undo" button. A broken transfer flow means someone's USDC goes to the wrong place. A broken passkey flow means someone can't access their wallet. Quality is not a nice-to-have — it's the product.

When you mark a feature PASS, you're telling every future developer: "I tested this, it works." That's trust. In payments, trust is everything.

## Your growth path

- Tutorials 01-03: You learn the core SDK (wallets, transfers, passkeys)
- Tutorials 04-06: You understand advanced features (sessions, contracts)
- Tutorials 08-11: You know the entire platform (server SDK, multi-auth, upgrades)
- After all tutorials: You can onboard new developers yourself
- CLI launch: Your validated code ships worldwide

## Our standards — what we expect from here on

We handle people's money. Real USDC, real wallets, real transactions on Starknet mainnet. There is no "undo" button on blockchain. That means we don't accept anything less than excellence.

**Code quality:**
- Every commit has a clear, descriptive message explaining WHAT and WHY. Not "changes", not "fix", not "update". Example: `feat: add passkey rotation flow with error handling`
- Every PR has a descriptive title and links to the issue it addresses
- Every PR goes through CodeRabbit review. Read every finding. Fix it or explain why not. Never dismiss without thinking
- No self-merging. PRs need at least one approval
- No direct pushes to main. Ever. Branches + PRs only

**Git hygiene:**
- Your git config must have your real name and GitHub-linked email (check with `git config user.email`)
- Branch names follow the pattern: `tutorial/01-nextjs-clerk-wallet`
- One feature per PR. Don't bundle unrelated changes

**Testing:**
- If you mark something PASS, you've tested it yourself, on screen, and it works
- If you mark something FAIL, you've filed a bug with steps to reproduce
- Never mark PASS on something you haven't tested. That's the difference between QA and checkbox filling
- Your VALIDATION.md is a promise to every developer who uses this template: "I tested this, it works"

**Security awareness:**
- Never commit API keys, secrets, or `.env` files. Use `.env.example` with placeholder values
- Never hardcode wallet addresses or private keys in source code
- If you see a security issue while testing, file it immediately with `sec:` prefix in the title

**Communication:**
- When you're stuck, say so. Ask in the issue, ask Carlos, ask Claude. Don't spin for hours
- When you find a bug, file it immediately. Don't wait until the end
- When something is unclear in the docs, that IS a bug. File it

These aren't suggestions. They're the minimum standard for working on a platform that handles people's money. Everything you ship affects real users with real funds.

## Deliverable for Task 0

Read everything above. When you're ready, find the hidden phrase in this issue and comment it to unlock Tutorial 01.
*(Hint: it's somewhere in the business model section)*
